login.title: Sign in to continue learning
login.subtitle: Signing in prepares the app for learner profiles, personalized roadmaps, test results, and lesson progress. This version uses localStorage, but the architecture is separated so Firebase or another database can be added later with minimal UI changes.
benefits: UI, Context, and Auth Provider are separated
The local provider can be replaced by Firebase
Pages do not need major changes when the backend changes
login.futureTitle: Designed for a real backend later
login.futureDesc: Pages only call AuthContext. When Firebase, Supabase, or a custom API is added, create a provider with the same login, register, logout, and getCurrentUser methods.
login.formSubtitle: Use an existing account or try the demo account below.
login.forgotHint: Password recovery will be handled after a real backend is connected.

register.title: Create learner profile
register.subtitle: A local account lets you test authentication, profiles, and learner grouping before connecting a real backend.
register.name: Full name
register.sideTitle: A clean auth flow for long-term growth
register.sideDesc: The registration flow is written in a frontend production-ready direction: form validation, local password hashing, service/context separation, and easy migration to a real backend.
benefits: Stores learner metadata: name, email, and learner group
Local passwords are hashed with the Web Crypto API
Session state is managed through AuthContext
Can be extended with admin, teacher, or parent roles later

profile.subtitle: This account information is stored locally to test the sign-in flow and learner experience.
profile.accountDesc: You can edit the name and learner group. Email is used as the sign-in identifier.
profile.name: Full name
profile.localModeDesc: Data is stored in this browser. This mode is useful for developing UI, testing the sign-in flow, and preparing the app before connecting a real backend.
futureItems: When Firebase is added, replace localAuthProvider with firebaseAuthProvider
Components still use useAuth, so UI changes are minimal
Roles such as admin, teacher, and parent can be added later
Thinking test and placement results can be saved by userId
profile.nextActionDesc: After signing in, learners can take the Thinking Test or Placement Test so the system can recommend a suitable roadmap.

errors.required: Please fill in all required fields.
errors.invalid_email: Please enter a valid email address.
errors.password_short: Password should be at least 8 characters.
errors.not_authenticated: You need to sign in first.
errors.crypto_unavailable: This browser does not support Web Crypto API for local password handling.
errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
